About: It is about the building of railroad corporations during the 19th century.
Affects: It affects the game mechanics and titling nomenclature.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with railroad corporations.
ConsistsOf: It consists of cash, listed company shares, and other investments.
CreatedAround: It was created around the mid-1970s.
CreatedBy: It was created by Francis Tresham.
HasA: It has a hex grid map.
It has hexagonal track tiles.
It has tokens to represent stations.
It has cities with values.
It has different color tiles available in succession.
It has trains that eventually become obsolete.
It has private companies.
It has minor companies.
It has company types.
It has corporate mergers and demergers.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by multiplayer board games.
It is characterized by the absence of random variables in game mechanics.
It is characterized by the objective to enhance personal wealth.
It is characterized by players indirectly interacting with the game board through companies they control.
It is characterized by a certificate limit.
It is characterized by a President's certificate.
It is characterized by restrictions on the order in which companies may be started.
It is characterized by scarcity of available corporations, shares, train types, and track tiles.
It is characterized by game end determined by the bank running out of money or player bankruptcy.
HeldIn: It was held in Chattanooga, Tennessee.
IsA: It is a generic term for a series of board games.
It is a series of board games.
KnownFor: It is known for recreating the building of railroad corporations during the 19th century.
It is known for its game mechanics and titling nomenclature.
It is known for its origins in the game 1829.
It is known for being widely available in the United States.
It is known for spawning self-published variants.
It is known for its common features.
It is known for its differences in rule set and game map.
It is known for conventions with an emphasis on these games.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the United States.
It occurred in the European Union.
PartOf: It is part of the 18XX titles.
It is part of the series.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Avalon Hill.
PublishedAs: It was published as self-published variants.
It was published as games by low-volume game companies.
UsedAs: It is used as a generic term.
It is used as the president of a company.
It is used as a title.
It is used as a hex grid.
It is used as track tiles.
It is used as station tokens.
It is used as a President's certificate.
It is used as a Director's certificate.
It is used as a hex.
It is used as a map.
UsedFor: It is used for building railroad corporations.
It is used for buying and selling stock.
It is used for laying track.
It is used for placing station tokens.
It is used for operating trains.
It is used for withholding income.
It is used for paying dividends.
It is used for buying trains.
It is used for enhancing personal wealth.
It is used for controlling companies.
UsedIn: It is used in the game 1829.
It is used in the game 1830.
It is used in the game 2038.
It is used in the game Poseidon.
It is used in the game Ur, 1830 BC.
It is used in the European Union.
It is used in the United States.
It is used in ancient history.
It is used in the game 1835.
It is used in the game 1861.
UsedTo: It is used to recreate the building of railroad corporations.
It is used to determine the title of individual games.
It is used to represent the growth of railroad networks.
It is used to represent stations.
It is used to keep the game competitive.
It is used to represent control of a railroad corporation.
It is used to impose a historical context upon the game.
It is used to determine the game end.
It is used to provide special abilities.
It is used to enhance the revenue of certain hexes.
